message:
feat(fwc): Implement live FCP host integration for batch execution and cancel operations

Upgrades fwc from planning/preview mode to true execution mode against a
live fcp-host instance, completing the host-backed CLI story.

Key additions:
- BatchOp gains an optional zone field for per-operation zone overrides,
  enabling fine-grained multi-zone batch workflows
- cancel_dispatch() with full reason/cleanup validation supporting five
  cancellation reasons (user-requested, agent-abort, timeout-approaching,
  resource-limit, superseded, session-closing) and four cleanup behaviors
  (best-effort, full, abandon, checkpoint), plus context-specific args
  (--remaining-ms, --resource, --current, --limit)
- map_dispatch() and batch_file_dispatch() now execute live batch invocations
  with per-operation schema validation against introspected schemas, optional
  --dry-run preflight authorization checks, and proper exit codes reflecting
  actual execution state
- Nine new helper functions: append_history_entry, parse_operation_reference,
  resolve_host_operation_from_catalog, build_live_invoke_request,
  build_host_batch_options, validate_payload_against_schema,
  preflight_status_label, batch_status_label, and error dispatch functions
- execute_serve_mcp() now passes resolved host context to MCP tool handlers
  for zone inheritance during tool call execution

All changes are additive — no existing functionality is removed or reverted.
Status responses change from "planned" to "ok|error|partial" with real data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
